text: The 'order of magnitude' complexity claim is asserted, not demonstrated — minimal Hugin worker vs maximal Scion is not a fair comparison
The 'conceptual mismatch' argument is too absolute — Scion can handle short-lived tasks
GRIMNIR_DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md makes Hugin look like a Scion reimplementation — worktrees, parallelism, TaskRunner, 3D state are all Scion features
Two Hugin instances polling Munin is a bespoke scheduler protocol, not 'just polling'
Migration cost from Hugin-worker to Scion later is understated — conventions harden quickly
Using present-day scale to reject Scion while planning next-scale orchestration in Hugin is internally conflicted
If Solo mode is the fair comparison, Hub duplication is a contingent concern not a settled strike
Decision trigger '<30 min setup' is too narrow — should evaluate state integration, failure handling, operational burden
Decoupling patterns from platform weakens Path A's strategic edge — makes the spike more important
